articles.title: New international student permit approvals for Canada fell below COVID levels in 2025
articles.summary: Canada approved 75,372 new study permits for international students in 2025. This figure represents a 64% year-over-year decline and an 18% decrease from the 2020 pandemic low.
articles.insight:
Canada’s sharp 64% YoY drop in new study permits—falling even below 2020 pandemic lows—may reflect tightened immigration policies, reduced post-study work incentives, or shifting perceptions among key source markets, though it’s unclear how much this signals broader global demand shifts versus Canada-specific factors.  
Admissions teams should track real-time application trends and sentiment data from overlapping source countries (e.g., India, Nigeria, Philippines), while reviewing their own visa support services and post-graduation outcome messaging to ensure competitiveness amid evolving student priorities.

articles.title: Number of Hong Kong school leavers seeking education elsewhere hits 14-year low
articles.summary: The number of Hong Kong secondary school leavers pursuing higher education outside the city dropped to 2,671 in 2025, representing 6.5% of the 40,948 Form Six graduates enrolled in full-time courses, marking a 13% decline from 2024’s figure of 3,061 and the lowest level in 14 years. An expert cited increased competition from mainland Chinese students applying to local universities as a key factor behind this trend.
articles.insight:
Hong Kong’s sharp 13% drop in outbound secondary leavers—reaching a 14-year low—may reflect shifting preferences toward mainland Chinese universities, though it’s unclear whether this signals broader regional demand softening or is specific to Hong Kong’s evolving education policy and cross-border mobility trends.  
Admissions teams should monitor Hong Kong’s Form Six cohort size, local university intake capacity, and mainland competition dynamics—noting potential implications for mid-tier applicants—while avoiding overreaction given the data’s narrow scope and possible short-term policy influences.

articles.title: UK: 7 in 10 universities report declining international postgraduate enrolments; visa rejections are part of the story
articles.summary: A recent survey by the British Universities International Liaison Association (BUILA) found that 70% of UK universities reported a decline in international postgraduate enrolments in January 2026 compared with January 2025, with an average reduction of 31%. The declines were particularly pronounced among students from “high-risk” markets, and visa rejections are cited as a contributing factor.
articles.insight:
This UK enrolment decline—driven partly by visa rejections and concentrated in high-risk markets—may signal growing student sensitivity to immigration policy uncertainty, though it’s unclear whether this reflects broader global demand shifts or UK-specific friction.  
Admissions teams should monitor visa refusal trends and sentiment shifts in overlapping source markets (e.g., India, Nigeria), while reviewing their own pre-application support, documentation guidance, and partnerships with trusted agents to mitigate similar administrative barriers.
